message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` README — specifically the undocumented `ItemExtensions.GetInputText()` extension method and the new `resolveReferences` parameter on `GetInputItemsAsync`/`GetInputTextAsync`, introduced by PR #57974.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (version not specified; triggered by commit a9a95cda / PR #57974)
**Affected API:** `ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable<Item>)`, `ResponseContext.GetInputItemsAsync(bool resolveReferences)`, `ResponseContext.GetInputTextAsync(bool resolveReferences)`
**Scenarios:**
- Developers calling `GetInputItemsAsync` or `GetInputTextAsync` are unaware of the `resolveReferences` parameter and its semantic impact (whether `ItemReferenceParam` entries are resolved or forwarded as-is)
- Developers needing to extract text from a pre-fetched item sequence cannot discover `ItemExtensions.GetInputText()` through the README
- The README does not explain the distinction between `GetInputTextAsync` (text shortcut) and `GetInputItemsAsync` (raw item access for multi-modal input)

**Root ask:** Update the README Key concepts / Examples sections to document the new extension method and the `resolveReferences` parameter, and add a corresponding snippet test.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation issue, not a runtime bug. The new APIs were added in PR #57974 and are already in the CHANGELOG; the gap is that the README was only partially updated.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #57974 to understand the full scope of the API changes introduced (return type change on `GetInputItemsAsync`, new `resolveReferences` parameter, new `ItemExtensions` class).
2. Follow the Implementation Guide in the issue body: insert the new `ResponseContext — input access` subsection into the README, add a snippet test in `tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs`, and run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver`.
3. Validate with `dotnet build`, `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live`, and `dotnet format` for the `agentserver` service directory.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — The package `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` lives under `sdk/agentserver/` and is a non-management client library.
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — Directly maps to the `sdk/agentserver/` service directory per CODEOWNERS (line 85).
- **Confidence:** High — The issue explicitly names the package and service directory with no ambiguity between services or categories.
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — the predicted service label "AI Agents" matches this entry exactly.
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ankitbko, @RaviPidaparthi
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; @mentioned ServiceOwners via routing comment. Issue left unassigned (no AzureSdkOwners). "needs-team-attention" not added because this is a bot-filed issue (no "customer-reported" label).
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top; management-specific entries (lines 900+) all require "Mgmt" — skipped. The `%AI Agents` entry at line 85 was the first match. No closer or more specific multi-label entry for "AI Agents" + "Client" exists in the file.
</details>
